ground_truth.title: Consulting Is More Than Giving Advice
ground_truth.author: Arthur N. Turner
ground_truth.main_content: Each year management consultants in the United States receive more than $2 billion for their services. Much of this money pays for impractical data and poorly implemented recommendations. To reduce this waste, clients need a better understanding of what consulting assignments can accomplish. They need to ask more from such advisers, who in turn must learn to satisfy expanded expectations.
with: Each year management consultants in the United States receive more than $2 billion for their services.
Much of this money pays for impractical data and poorly implemented recommendations.
To reduce this waste, clients need a better understanding of what consulting assignments can accomplish.
They need to ask more from such advisers, who in turn must learn to satisfy expanded expectations.
By building on a hierarchy of goals, consultants and managers can work toward mutual interests.
